title: When the fusion is right and the collaboration is wrong
why: Any toolkit for noticing fusion assumes the collaboration is the good and the fusion is the obstacle. Sometimes that's inverted. A tool that can only ever recommend more dialogue is a compliance instrument with good manners on it.
question: If fusion can be the correct response to something genuinely threatening, how does a tool for noticing it avoid becoming a device for talking people out of warranted loyalty?
fallback: Build the detection side only, self-facing, with no recommendation layer at all. It notices heat and names it. What you do next stays entirely with the person holding it.
flagNote: Every one of these smuggles in a verdict about whose commitments are legitimate.
